overview: A five-color combo deck that leverages Indomitable Creativity to draw cards and cast spells for free, accelerating into game-winning sequences. Prismari Command provides flexible draw and land acceleration, while Spell Pierce protects the combo from disruption. The deck aims to assemble its pieces quickly and overwhelm opponents before they can stabilize.
win_conditions: Casting a high-impact spell for free via Indomitable Creativity to close the game
Drawing into a lethal combination of cheap spells and creatures
Using Prismari Command to fetch lands and draw into the combo package

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two mana sources and a draw engine
Hands containing Indomitable Creativity or Prismari Command with early interaction
Hands with Spell Pierce and a cheap spell to protect the combo
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including fetches/shocks), at least one cantrip or discard, and early interaction (Spell Pierce, Thoughtseize, or cheap removal).
Prioritize hands that can play a land and a spell on turn 1.
If you have Creativity, you need at least two cheap spells or cantrips to enable it.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking mana acceleration or draw
Hands with only high-cost spells and no protection
Hands without interaction against early aggression
Mulligan hands with 0-1 lands, multiple high-cost spells, or Creativity with no support.

early_game: Play lands and cheap spells to establish board presence
Use Spell Pierce to counter key removal or counterspells
Look for early Prismari Command to draw into Creativity
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a cantrip (Opt, Ponder, Brainstorm) or discard to dig for Creativity and interaction.
Turn 2: Cast another cantrip, play a land, or cast a cheap spell (Lightning Bolt, Go Blank, Prismari Command) to fill the graveyard.
If you draw Creativity early, protect it immediately with counterspells or discard.
Sequence mana to ensure you can cast multiple spells per turn once Creativity is active.
mid_game: Cast Indomitable Creativity to draw two and cast a finisher for free
Maintain card advantage by cycling through cheap spells
Apply pressure with creatures while protecting combo pieces
always keep enough for multiple spells per turn once Creativity is active.
prioritize filling the graveyard with cheap spells and cantrips.
Use discard to disrupt opponent's hand while building your own graveyard.
Never overextend mana if you lack Creativity or interaction.
late_game: Execute free spell sequences to deplete opponent's resources
Use excess card draw to find answers to board wipes
Close out games with unanswerable threats or direct damage
A combination of a large creature (e.g., Kethis, the Hidden Hand, or a big spell like Storm the Festival) cast from the graveyard, or a direct damage/combo finisher.
The goal is to cast multiple spells in a single turn to overwhelm the board and close the game before the opponent can stabilize.
Go all-in when you have Creativity active, a full graveyard, and enough interaction to protect your board or answer their response.
If you can cast 3+ spells this turn and close the game, commit.
Do not hold back if you have the resources to win this turn.

key_cards.name: Indomitable Creativity
key_cards.notes: Cast to draw two cards and cast a nonland card for free; prioritize high-impact spells or finishers.

key_cards.name: Prismari Command
key_cards.notes: Use to draw cards, fetch dual lands, or tap lands for mana to enable Creativity.

key_cards.name: Spell Pierce
key_cards.notes: Hold up to counter removal, counterspells, or key threats that would stall the combo.

sequencing_tips: Hold Spell Pierce up until the opponent reveals their hand or commits to a spell
Cast Prismari Command before Indomitable Creativity to ensure you have enough cards to choose from
Prioritize casting cheap spells before Creativity to maximize the free cast potential
Common mistake: Overcommitting to the graveyard without protecting Creativity.
Unique interaction: Creativity lets you cast spells from the graveyard, but you still can't cast spells from your hand.
Use this to your advantage by keeping your hand empty of spells to avoid accidentally casting them.
Also, note that Creativity doesn't bypass counterspells or removal, so sequencing is critical.
Prismari Command is a versatile tool for drawing cards, countering, or dealing damage, so use it flexibly based on the board state.

matchups.advice: Use Spell Pierce to counter early threats and buy time for Creativity
Play around counterspells by baiting them with cheap spells before casting Creativity
Apply steady pressure while protecting combo pieces with interaction

Creativity bypasses hand restrictions, but counters still work. Use cheap spells to trade, cast Creativity in response to their counters, or use discard to remove their counterspells. Prioritize casting spells when they have no mana up. Use Prismari Command to counter or draw into answers.

Out-value them with card draw and discard. Use early interaction to slow their mana development. Once Creativity is active, cast multiple spells to race their big threats. Use discard to remove their ramp spells and counterspells.

Win the graveyard war. Prioritize discard to empty their graveyard, protect your Creativity, and out-value them with better sequencing. Use counterspells to protect your key spells. Track their graveyard to know when to strike.

common_threats: Watch for graveyard hate (Rest in Peace, Leyline of the Void, Surgical Extraction), sweepers, and counterspells.
Note when opponents start holding up interaction for Creativity.
Track their graveyard if they play graveyard-based strategies.
Pay attention to mana development to predict when they can cast their key spells.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: 5c Indomitable Creativity, Prismari Command, Spell Pierce

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Spell Pierce, Prismari Command, Indomitable Creativity
